# OpenAI Abruptly Shutters Sora Video Generator, Scrapping $1 Billion Disney Deal. In a sudden and significant strategic pivot, OpenAI has announced the discontinuation of its flagship AI video generation tool, Sora, just over a year after its public debut. **Terminated Disney-OpenAI Deal (Announced Dec. 2025):**. * **Investment:** $1 billion USD equity investment from Disney to OpenAI (reportedly never finalized). * **Licensing:** Disney granted OpenAI access to over 200 copyrighted characters for use in Sora and ChatGPT. * **Status:** Deal terminated following OpenAI's decision to shutter the Sora platform. ### OpenAI Announces Strategic Shift Away from Video Generation. On March 24, 2026, OpenAI declared it was "saying goodbye to Sora," marking the end of its foray into consumer-facing AI video generation. The shutdown encompasses the standalone Sora application, its developer API, and video generation features within ChatGPT. That agreement granted OpenAI access to over 200 Disney characters for use in Sora and ChatGPT, and included a planned $1 billion USD equity investment from the entertainment giant into OpenAI. Internal shifts at OpenAI, including a reported "red alert" from CEO Sam Altman in late December 2025 urging focus on core priorities, left the deal in limbo before it was formally scrapped. Internally, Sora was reportedly a massive consumer of expensive and scarce computing power, drawing resources away from other products. Externally, OpenAI faces fierce competition from rivals like Anthropic, whose Claude model has been gaining significant traction in the enterprise market and even overtook ChatGPT as the most downloaded AI app in the U.S. recently. ### OpenAI's New Focus: The "Superapp" and Enterprise AI. With Sora out of the picture, OpenAI's strategic direction is becoming clearer. * **Discontinued:** All Sora products (consumer app, API, ChatGPT integration). ### The Legacy of Sora and the AI Video Wars. Sora's shutdown concludes a controversial chapter in generative AI. While Sora demonstrated remarkable technical capability, its closure suggests that the market for standalone AI video generation tools—amid legal, ethical, and commercial challenges—may be more precarious than initially believed. OpenAI Exits AI Video Generation Entirely. The highest-profile AI video product lasted just six months. OpenAI Exits AI Video Generation Entirely. OpenAI is walking away from AI video entirely. Announced in December 2025 with a reported $1 billion investment, the deal would have let users generate content featuring Disney's 200+ iconic characters through Sora. It is a signal that the standalone AI video app model faces serious challenges. Building a dedicated platform for AI video generation requires massive GPU infrastructure with thin margins. You might expect the DALL-E playbook here: shut down the standalone app, fold video generation into ChatGPT, reach 900 million weekly active users. Earlier reports in March suggested OpenAI was planning a ChatGPT integration, but the March 24 announcement made clear the company is exiting video generation, not relocating it. The standalone AI video app model just took a serious hit. This does not mean AI video products are doomed. Standalone consumer video apps with high compute costs, broad general-purpose video platforms without a niche, tools that compete purely on generation quality. # OpenAI Is Shutting Down Sora AI Video Generation App—Is It Because of the Rumored ChatGPT Video? #### Sora is bidding farewell soon, but is it because of ChatGPT? OpenAI has revealed that it is planning to shut down its AI video generation app, Sora, after a little over two years since it was launched without really explaining the reason why. That being said, there had been various reports and rumors earlier this year that talked about ChatGPT getting specific video generation features from Sora, similar to what it received from DALL-E before. ## OpenAI Is Shutting Down the Sora AI Video App. The Sora Team under OpenAI recently shared an announcement on their official X account, which confirmed their plans to shut down the Sora app in the near future. The team thanked everyone who has been part of the Sora platform, including those who used the generative AI platform to create videos, shared their output online, and joined its community. The Sora team also said that they will soon share information about timelines for the app, its API, and details on preserving user works. The Sora Team said that this move is "disappointing," but shared no details behind its sunsetting. It also came after Disney's $1 billion investment in OpenAI, which also included plans to bring Disney's characters to Sora. > We’re saying goodbye to Sora. To everyone who created with Sora, shared it, and built community around it: thank you. What you made with Sora mattered, and we know this news is disappointing. > We’ll share more soon, including timelines for the app and API and details on…. While there are no direct explanations from OpenAI and the Sora Team, a previous report from The Information talked about ChatGPT getting AI video generation features right on its platform. The report said that this would be similar to getting AI photo generation from DALL-E. It remains unconfirmed if OpenAI is transforming ChatGPT into an all-in-one app for all generative AI creations, but there are talks about the company creating a "super app" that will combine the chatbot, its browser, Codex, and more in a single platform.","score":0.82728493,"raw_content":null,"domain":"hngn.com","popularity_score":2.4849066497880004,"main_topic":"news"},{"url":"https://thepulsegazette.com/article/openai-shuts-down-sora-video-tool-months-after-launch","title":"OpenAI Shuts Down Sora Video Tool Months After Launch - The Pulse Gazette","content":"OpenAI is killing its standalone Sora video generator less than 10 months after launch, folding the tool into ChatGPT and sunsetting the separate website and API by **March 31, 2026**. Sora debuted in December 2024 to considerable fanfare — OpenAI's first text-to-video model capable of generating 60-second clips with "complex scenes and multiple characters," according to its launch materials. OpenAI told users the "core Sora technology" will survive inside ChatGPT, where it can reach the platform's **400 million weekly active users** rather than the comparatively tiny audience that signed up for a specialized video tool. Enterprise customers who'd integrated Sora into production workflows — including several advertising agencies and short-form content studios — must rebuild on Runway, Kling, or OpenAI's still-limited ChatGPT API video capabilities. Early user feedback on the integrated Sora suggests video generation feels "buried" in the chat interface, requiring multiple clicks to access and lacking the project management features of the standalone tool.","score":0.8259413,"raw_content":null,"domain":"thepulsegazette.com","popularity_score":0.26236426446749106,"main_topic":"news"}],"response_time":2.33,"request_id":"8e6877cf-09eb-4c66-95c7-5b130f833bce"}
